informal_statement.text: Given a slice of integers, return a new vector containing the same multiset of elements in nondecreasing order.
preconditions: the input is a slice of i32 values of any length
required_properties: the output length equals the input length
the output is sorted in nondecreasing order
the output is a permutation of the input (same multiset of elements)
edge_cases: slice of length that is a power of two
slice of length that is not a power of two

semantic_units.description: Length preservation: the output has the same length as the input.
common_failure_modes: stating only a length bound instead of equality

semantic_units.description: Sortedness: the output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
Permutation: the output is a permutation of the input (equal multiset of elements).
Stability is NOT required by the spec.

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output length equals the input length.
The output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
The output is a permutation of the input.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit preconditions are required by the implementation; merge_sort is defined for all input lists/arrays.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (arr : List Int), WellFounded (measure (fun l => l.length)) (fun l => if l.length ≤ 1 then [] else [l.take (l.length/2), l.drop (l.length/2)])
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The recursive calls in merge_sort strictly decrease the input length (split into two smaller halves), ensuring termination.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (left right : List Int), Sorted (merge left right)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The merge procedure outputs a sorted list when given two sorted input lists.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (left right : List Int), Permutation (merge left right) (left ++ right)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The merge procedure outputs a permutation of the concatenation of its inputs (it only reorders elements, does not create or drop elements).

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (left right : List Int), Sorted left → Sorted right → StableMerge left right (merge left right)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Stability: when elements are equal, merge takes from the left first (because it uses <=), preserving relative order of equal elements across left/right.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (left right : List Int), (merge left right).length = left.length + right.length
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: The merged output length equals the sum of input lengths (elements are neither lost nor duplicated).
merge_sort returns a permutation of the input (same multiset of elements).
Stability of merge_sort: because it uses a stable merge at each step, the overall sort is stable.
merge_sort preserves length (no elements are added or removed).
